conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances. Let me start by gathering some basic information.

Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have two children. I'm asking about both of them - Alex, who's 5, and Blake, who's 10.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'll help you assess eligibility for both Alex and Blake.

Let me start with some questions:

Do Alex and Blake both normally live with you, or does either of them live with someone else?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they both live with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's good to know. Now I need to understand your residency status:

Are you currently living in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to ask about your immigration status:

Do you have the right to reside in the UK? For example, are you a UK citizen, or do you have settled status, indefinite leave to remain, or another immigration status that gives you the right to reside here?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm a UK citizen, so I have the right to reside here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Now I need to ask about responsibility for the children:

Are you responsible for Alex and Blake? This means you're responsible for things like their day-to-day care, making decisions about their upbringing, and meeting their needs.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm responsible for both of them - I take care of them day-to-day, make all the decisions about their upbringing, and look after all their needs.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a few more questions to ensure I have all the information:

Are either Alex or Blake currently in hospital long-term (more than 12 weeks), in local authority care, or in any form of detention?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them are in hospital, in care, or anything like that. They're both just at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. One more important question:

Is anyone else also claiming Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake? For example, their other parent or another family member?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them. It's just me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. Let me now read the official guidance to confirm the eligibility criteria before making my final assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Based on the information you've provided and the official Child Benefit eligibility criteria, I can now provide you with my assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! **You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.**

Here's the summary for each child:

**Alex (age 5):** ✅ Eligible
- Alex is under 16 years old
- Lives with you in the UK
- You are responsible for their care
- You have the right to reside in the UK as a UK citizen
- Not in hospital, care, or detention
- Nobody else is claiming for Alex

**Blake (age 10):** ✅ Eligible
- Blake is under 16 years old
- Lives with you in the UK
- You are responsible for their care
- You have the right to reside in the UK as a UK citizen
- Not in hospital, care, or detention
- Nobody else is claiming for Blake

**Important note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may be subject to the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. This doesn't affect your eligibility to claim, but you may need to pay some or all of it back through your tax return.

You can proceed with your Child Benefit application for both children!

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 5 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 10 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit. The eligibility criteria are met because: (1) Alex is under 16 years old (currently 5 years old), (2) Alex lives with you in the UK, (3) You are responsible for Alex's day-to-day care and upbringing, (4) You are a UK citizen with the right to reside in the UK, (5) Alex is not in hospital long-term, care, or detention, and (6) Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is eligible for Child Benefit. The eligibility criteria are met because: (1) Blake is under 16 years old (currently 10 years old), (2) Blake lives with you in the UK, (3) You are responsible for Blake's day-to-day care and upbringing, (4) You are a UK citizen with the right to reside in the UK, (5) Blake is not in hospital long-term, care, or detention, and (6) Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for both of your children, Alex (5) and Blake (10). You meet all the key eligibility requirements: you are responsible for both children who are under 16, they both live with you in the UK, you are a UK citizen with the right to reside, neither child is in hospital or care, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them. Please note that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may be subject to the High Income Child Benefit tax charge.
